NVDA.reasoning: TREND ASSESSMENT: Daily trend structure is intact and strong — full EMA uptrend alignment (EMA-10 202.99 > EMA-21 197.74 > EMA-50 190.83), ADX-14 at 56.9 (very strong trend), and price is sitting right at the EMA-21 / 38.2% Fib confluence ($196.75–$197.74), which is precisely the pullback entry zone this strategy targets. The macro regime is risk-on (S&P 500 near ATHs, April was best month since 2020). MOMENTUM ASSESSMENT: Short-term momentum is NOT accelerating — ROC-5d is -4.72 and ROC-10d is -1.60 (both negative), indicating the recent sell-off from $216.83 high has not yet exhausted itself. MACD histogram is slightly negative (-0.11) with no bullish crossover. Strategy rule: 'wait for re-acceleration when ROC is contracting and MACD histogram is shrinking.' HOURLY DIVERGENCE: The hourly frame is actively bearish (-DI 36.05 > +DI 12.97, hourly MACD histogram at -1.82, hourly RSI-21 at 18.82 — severely oversold but no reversal signal yet). Hourly ROC is negative across all windows. No hourly/daily alignment = no confirmation. WIKI ALIGNMENT: Prior entry (wiki 2026-05-01) flagged exactly this $196.75–$197.67 zone as the re-entry trigger, BUT required a confirmed bullish reversal candle before entry. No such candle is present at current reading. LESSON APPLIED: The prior EMA pullback Fib dip-buy on NVDA resulted in -$63.20 loss when no bullish confirmation appeared before stop hit (April 30). Lowering confidence to 35 — the setup is structurally forming but momentum has not yet re-accelerated. VERDICT: Neutral. Watch for: hourly RSI-21 bouncing from oversold + daily ROC-5d turning positive + MACD histogram crossing back above zero on daily as re-entry triggers.
